summary: Replace `SELECT DISTINCT ... ORDER BY captured_at DESC` with correlated subquery selecting the latest snapshot per playlist.
reason: SQLite applies ORDER BY before DISTINCT, so the captured_at ordering hint was silently dropped — a track in 10 historical snapshots of the same playlist generated 10 rows in undefined order, not 1 latest row. Greptile review caught this. The correlated subquery filters to MAX(captured_at) per playlist for the given track, matching the documented 'where is this track right now' semantic.
